preamble: The voiceless uvular plosive /q/ can undergo a historical sound change to the voiced alveolar plosive /d/. This shift involves significant changes in both the place of articulation and voicing.
phoneticEffects: This transformation entails a fronting of the place of articulation from uvular to alveolar, and a change in voicing from voiceless to voiced

examples.note: A regular historical shift from /q/ to /d/ is attested in Hebrew.

sources: Blau, J. (1978a). Topics in Hebrew and Semitic Linguistics. Magnes Press.
